titlStmt.titl: Codebook for an AHTUS Data Extract
titlStmt.subTitl: DDI 2.5 metadata describing the extract file 'ahtus_00002.dat'

prodStmt.prodPlac: IPUMS, 50 Willey Hall, 225 - 19th Avenue South, Minneapolis, MN 55455
IPUMS, 50 Willey Hall, 225 - 19th Avenue South, Minneapolis, MN 55455

stdyInfo.notes: Additional notes on a sample that is part of this study: 1930

useStmt.citReq:
Publications and research reports based on the AHTUS database must cite it appropriately. The citation should include the following:
Kimberly Fisher, Jonathan Gershuny, Sarah M. Flood, Joan Garcia Roman, and Sandra L. Hofferth. American Heritage Time Use Study Extract Builder: Version 1.2 [dataset]. Minneapolis, MN: IPUMS, 2018. https://doi.org/10.18128/D061.V1.2
The licensing agreement for use of AHTUS data requires that users supply us with the title and full citation for any publications, research reports, or educational materials making use of the data or documentation. Please add your citation to the IPUMS bibliography: http://bibliography.ipums.org/
useStmt.conditions:
Users of AHTUS data must agree to abide by the conditions of use. A user's license is valid for one year and may be renewed. Users must agree to the following conditions:
(1) No fees may be charged for use or distribution of the data. All persons are granted a limited license to use these data, but you may not charge a fee for the data if you distribute it to others.
(2) Cite IPUMS appropriately. For information on proper citation, refer to the citation requirement section of this DDI document.
(3) Tell us about any work you do using the IPUMS. Publications, research reports, or presentations making use of AHTUS should be added to our Bibliography. Continued funding for the IPUMS depends on our ability to show our sponsor agencies that researchers are using the data for productive purposes.
(4) Use it for GOOD -- never for EVIL.
useStmt.disclaimer: The user of the data acknowledges that the original collector of the data, the authorized distributor of the data, and the relevant funding agency bear no responsibility for use of the data or for interpretations or inferences based upon such uses.

stdyDscr.notes: User-provided description: Revision to add DAY var
This extract is a revision of the user's previous extract, ID 5547808.

fileTxt.filePlac: IPUMS, 50 Willey Hall, 225 - 19th Avenue South, Minneapolis, MN 55455

var.name: SAMPLE
var.txt: SAMPLE indicates the dataset to which the case belongs. Some surveys include data from more than one year (YEAR) and other datasets combine respondents from more than one survey (SURVEY).

var.name: PERNUM
var.txt: PERNUM is constructed to uniquely identify each person within a given household. It is available for all household members whether they were present in the household at the time of the survey. This is the only line number variable available for each person in every household.
var.codInstr: This is a 2-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: IDENT
var.txt: IDENT is an identifier to link person and activity records. It is unique within but not across surveys. Together IDENT and SAMPLE uniquely identify persons.
var.codInstr: This is a 9-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: HHID
var.txt:
HHID reports the household identifier in the original data.
Note that in datasets that include more than one diary per person and/or more than one person per household, HHID is not unique. Use IDENT to identify uniquely cases within a dataset and to link persons and activities records in hierarchical files.
var.codInstr: This is a 16-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: PID
var.txt:
PID indicates the person identifier in the original dataset.
Note that in datasets that include more than one diary per person and/or more than one person per household, PID is not unique. Use IDENT to identify uniquely cases and link persons and activities records in hierarchical files.
var.codInstr: This is a 14-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: SERIAL
var.txt: SERIAL is an identifying number unique to each person.

var.name: YEAR
var.txt:
YEAR indicates the year the survey was completed.
Although in most surveys data were collected in the same year, others include diaries kept in different years. In 1965 and 1998 when diaries were combined from two different surveys (SURVEY) and collected in different years, YEAR and SAMPLE are not the same.
Detailed information about each sample is available elsewhere.

var.name: AGE
var.txt: AGE reports the age of respondents.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: SEX
var.txt: SEX reports whether the respondent is male or female.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: CIVSTAT
var.txt:
CIVSTAT reports the marital status of the respondent.
Users should note that some cohabiting but not legally married couples report themselves as married. Users may refer to COHAB for more detailed information.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: DIARYDAY
var.txt: DIARYDAY reports the day of the week when the diary was kept.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: EDUC
var.txt: EDUC reports the highest educational level of the respondent.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: RECWGHT
var.labl: Recommended sample (day) weight removing low quality diaries and missing age or sex
var.txt:
RECWGHT is the recommended sample weight.
This weight accounts for population/sample distribution by age group and sex, provides an even distribution of the days of the week (and corrects for the oversample of parents in the 1999-2001 element of the 1998-2001 combined survey) for good quality diaries.
In RECWGHT, all cases with missing basic information or bad diaries are 0-weighted, and thus are excluded from analysis. Nonetheless, these bad diaries remain in the files so that users who may examine the low-quality cases. For more information about the weights, see the weights section in the AHTUS user guide.
Cases were retained where demographic information exits for a spouse who did not complete a diary to allow users to examine non-response, but these cases were zero-weighted.

var.name: ETHNIC
var.txt: ETHNIC reports the detailed ethnic group of the respondent. Users who want a historically-compatible ethnicity variable should use ETHNIC2. In addition, users may also want to use HISP, which indicates Hispanic descent.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: AGEYNGST
var.txt:
AGEYNGST reports the age of the respondent's youngest child, who may or may not be a household member.
More detailed information about the number of children under 18 years old in the household is available in UNDER18. Similarly, FAMSTAT summarizes the household composition of the respondents.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: DISAB
var.txt: DISAB reports whether the respondent is disabled or has a long-term limiting health condition.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: REGIONC
var.txt: REGIONC reports the respondent's region of residence according to the Census classification.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: URBAN
var.txt:
URBAN reports whether the respondent resides in an urban or rural area.
This variable was provided in the original dataset for the 1965-66, 1975-76 and the 1985 surveys. For the 1992-1995 surveys, this variable was created using the zipcode of the respondent's home address. Please see documentation provided by Oxford's Centre for Time Use Research for more information. For the 2003+ survey years, URBAN is constructed using the 1990 definitions of Metropolitan status.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: HHTYPE
var.txt:
HHTYPE reports the household type.
Users may also refer to CIVSTAT for marital status of the respondents.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: NADULT
var.labl: Number of adults in the household
var.txt: NADULT reports the number of adults in the household.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: UNDER5
var.labl: Number of children under 5 in the household
var.txt: UNDER5 reports the number of children under 5 years old in the household. For the age of the respondent's youngest child see AGEYNGST.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: UNDER18
var.labl: Number of children under 18 in the household
var.txt: UNDER18 reports the number of children under 18 years old in the household. UNDER5 indicates the number of children under 5 years old in the household. For the age of the respondent's youngest child see AGEYNGST.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: EMPSTAT
var.txt:
EMPSTAT reports the employment status of the respondent.
Users may find other variables about employment useful: FULLTIME, PARTTIME, NOEMPLOY, and UNEMP.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: EMPSP
var.txt: EMPSP reports the employment status of the spouse or partner of the respondent.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: WAGELM
var.txt:
WAGELM reports the respondent's employment income from last month.
In 1975, the exact amount is reported.
In 1985, wage is calculated from the wage rate and weekly hours multiplied by 4.
In 2003, wages from last week multiplied by 4 is reported. Users interested in more detailed household income data may refer to INCOMEQT.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: WKHRS
var.labl: Number of hours worked per week
var.txt: WKHRS reports the number of hours the respondent worked per week.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on the case record

var.name: NOEMPLOY
var.txt:
NOEMPLOY reports whether the respondent is not employed.
Non-employment may be the result of retirement, being a student or homemaker status. It does not indicate unemployment but participation in the labor force. Users interested in more detailed non-employment status may refer to RETIRED, STUDENT, and HOMEMAKR

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: HOMEMAKR
var.txt:
HOMEMAKR reports whether the respondent is a homemaker.
Homemakers are categorized as not employed. Users may refer to NOEMPLOY for more detailed information.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: FULLTIME
var.txt:
FULLTIME reports whether the respondent works full time.
Users who want greater detail about respondents' employment may refer to EMPSTAT. Users who want to know the number of full-time workers in the household may refer to NWORK.
Users who want detailed information about part-time work may refer to PARTTIME, which reports whether the respondent works part-time.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: PARTTIME
var.txt:
PARTTIME reports whether the respondent works part time.
Users who want greater detail about respondents' employment may refer to EMPSTAT.
Users who want detailed information about full-time work may refer to FULLTIME, which reports whether the respondent works full time.

catgry.labl: Missing or dirty on case record

var.name: ACT_CHCARE
var.txt: ACT: Child care
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_CIVIC
var.labl: ACT: Adult care, civic, voluntary, and religious activities
var.txt: ACT: Adult care, civic, voluntary, and religious activities
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_EDUCA
var.txt: ACT: Education
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_INHOME
var.labl: ACT: In home free time leisure
var.txt: ACT: In home free time leisure
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_MEDIA
var.txt: ACT: Media and computing
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_MISSING
var.txt: ACT: Missing Activities
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_OUTHOME
var.labl: ACT: Out of home free time and leisure
var.txt: ACT: Out of home free time and leisure
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_PCARE
var.txt: ACT: Personal care
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_PHYSICAL
var.labl: ACT: Sports, exercise, and outdoor activities
var.txt: ACT: Sports, exercise, and outdoor activities
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_TRAVEL
var.txt: ACT: Travel
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_UNDOM
var.txt: ACT: Unpaid domestic work
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places

var.name: ACT_WORK
var.txt: ACT: Paid Work
var.codInstr: This is a 4-digit numeric variable with 0 implied decimal places
